notes: Grid search assumes a circular anomaly.
Differential mode compares hidden-minus-background residual traces and is usually less fooled by direct arrivals.
v0.4 adds staged search: center first, top-K radius/velocity search second, optional final local center refinement.
Joint strategy remains available for v0.3-style behavior and for diagnosing impostor solutions.
The mismatch map is taken from the final displayed search level; inspect search_levels for the full multi-stage trajectory.
The optional sponge boundary is a simple damping layer, not a production PML.
Center-only pass with nominal radius and velocity.
Local center refinement around top-K parent rank 1 from the previous center pool.
Local center refinement around top-K parent rank 2 from the previous center pool.
Local center refinement around top-K parent rank 3 from the previous center pool.
Local center refinement around top-K parent rank 4 from the previous center pool.
Local center refinement around top-K parent rank 5 from the previous center pool.
Radius/velocity search at refined center rank 1.
Radius/velocity search at refined center rank 2.
Radius/velocity search at refined center rank 3.
Radius/velocity search at refined center rank 4.
Radius/velocity search at refined center rank 5.
Final local center refinement using the best radius/velocity candidate.
These probabilities are derived from mismatch values, not a calibrated Bayesian posterior.
effective_candidates is exp(entropy): roughly how many candidates remain plausible under this soft weighting.
center_effective_candidates groups radius/velocity variants that share the same center.
High entropy means many candidates explain the traces about equally well.
